test_cases.test_case: Email with leading and trailing whitespace is submitted

test_cases.test_case: Press browser Back immediately after successful sign-in (rapid post-redirect back navigation)
preconditions: A registered user exists with Email/Username <registered email> and Password <valid password>
User has completed a successful sign-in in this browser session (saw 'Signed in successfully.' and is on Accounts Overview page)
steps: 1. From the Accounts Overview page after successful sign-in, press the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: succeeds: Pressing Back does not create a second successful sign-in; either the browser shows the Login page with Email/Username and Password empty or the browser returns to Accounts Overview without re-submitting credentials. No additional 'Signed in successfully.' flash appears and no duplicate session/entity is created

steps: 1. Select 'Custom date range' in the Statement_Period dropdown
2. Leave the Start_Date field blank
3. Enter <valid date> in the End_Date field
4. Click the 'Generate Statement' button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; Start_Date field displays an inline validation error indicating a start date is required to define the custom range (and to validate Start_Date <= End_Date); no 'Statement generated successfully.' message is shown and no statement is retrieved

test_cases.test_case: Generate Statement with Custom date range entering an invalid/impossible date
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Statements page
Generate Statement form is visible
steps: 1. Select 'Custom date range' in the Statement_Period dropdown
2. Enter <invalid date> in the Start_Date field
3. Enter <valid date> in the End_Date field
4. Click the 'Generate Statement' button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; Start_Date displays an inline validation error indicating the entered date is invalid; no 'Statement generated successfully.' message is shown and no statement is retrieved

test_cases.test_case: Generate Statement with Custom date range entering an invalid/impossible End_Date
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Statements page
Generate Statement form is visible
steps: 1. Select 'Custom date range' in the Statement_Period dropdown
2. Enter <valid date> in the Start_Date field
3. Enter <invalid date> in the End_Date field
4. Click the 'Generate Statement' button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; End_Date displays an inline validation error indicating the entered date is invalid; no 'Statement generated successfully.' message is shown and no statement is retrieved

test_cases.test_case: Custom range where Start_Date equals End_Date (boundary allowed)
preconditions: User is on the Statements page with Generate Statement form visible
steps: 1. Select 'Custom date range' in Statement_Period dropdown
2. Enter <date X> into Start_Date
3. Enter <date X> into End_Date
4. Click the 'Generate Statement' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission succeeds; 'Statement generated successfully.' is displayed and the relevant transactions for the single-day range are retrieved and shown

test_cases.test_case: Custom range where Start_Date is one day after End_Date (just past the allowed boundary)
preconditions: User is on the Statements page with Generate Statement form visible
steps: 1. Select 'Custom date range' in Statement_Period dropdown
2. Enter <date Y> into Start_Date
3. Enter <one day before Start_Date> into End_Date
4. Click the 'Generate Statement' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked; an inline validation error is shown on Start_Date or End_Date indicating Start_Date must be on or before End_Date (if the form-level submit fails, the page shows 'Unable to generate statement — please try again later.'), i.e. the attempt to generate is blocked / error shown

test_cases.test_case: Paperless enabled with Email_Address entered including leading and trailing whitespace (trim behavior)
preconditions: User is on the Statements page with E-Statement Preference form visible
steps: 1. Check the Paperless checkbox
2. Enter an otherwise valid email address with leading and trailing whitespace into Email_Address (e.g., ' <valid email> ')
3. Click the 'Save Preference' button
test_cases.expected_result: Save succeeds; 'e-Statement preference updated.' is displayed and the saved Email_Address shown in the preference summary/detail has leading and trailing whitespace removed (whitespace trimmed)

test_cases.test_case: Paperless enabled but Email_Address left blank (conditional required_when boundary)
preconditions: User is on the Statements page with E-Statement Preference form visible
steps: 1. Check the Paperless checkbox
2. Leave Email_Address empty
3. Click the 'Save Preference' button
test_cases.expected_result: Save is blocked; Email_Address field is highlighted with guidance (the form prevents save when Paperless == true and Email_Address is not provided)

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of 'Generate Statement' to test duplicate submission handling
preconditions: User is on the Statements page with Generate Statement form visible and a valid Statement_Period value entered (e.g., Month-and-year selected and Month_Year filled)
steps: 1. Ensure Statement_Period is set and Month_Year or date fields are populated as required
2. Click the 'Generate Statement' button twice in rapid succession (two immediate clicks)
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission is blocked; only one 'Statement generated successfully.' message is shown and only a single retrieval of transactions occurs (no duplicate statements are created or displayed)

test_cases.test_case: Switching Statement_Period from Month-and-year to Custom then using Start_Date == End_Date (visibility toggle + boundary)
preconditions: User is on the Statements page with Generate Statement form visible
steps: 1. Select 'Month-and-year' in Statement_Period dropdown
2. Enter a value into Month_Year
3. Change Statement_Period to 'Custom date range'
4. Enter <date Z> into Start_Date
5. Enter <date Z> into End_Date
6. Click the 'Generate Statement' button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission succeeds; 'Statement generated successfully.' is displayed and the relevant transactions for the chosen single-day custom range are retrieved and shown (the visibility toggle correctly revealed the date fields and the boundary date pair is accepted)

test_cases.test_case: Change password with valid current password and matching strong new password
preconditions: User logged in as <User>
Security Settings page is open
User's current password is known and correct (<current password>)
steps: 1. Expand the 'Change Password' panel if it is collapsed
2. Enter <current password> in the Current Password field
3. Enter <new strong password> in the New Password field (meets strong-password policy)
4. Enter <new strong password> in the Confirm New Password field (matches New Password)
5. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: A success notification is displayed with the text "Password changed successfully."

test_cases.test_case: Representative password field left blank (New_Password)
preconditions: User is on the Security Settings page and the Change Password form is visible
steps: 1. Leave the New_Password field blank
2. Enter <correct current password> in the Current_Password field
3. Enter <matching new password> in the Confirm_New_Password field
4. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: New_Password field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; the form does not submit; credentials are not updated and the page does not display 'Password changed successfully.'

test_cases.test_case: Submit with all required password fields empty
preconditions: User is on the Security Settings page and the Change Password form is visible
steps: 1. Leave the Current_Password field blank
2. Leave the New_Password field blank
3. Leave the Confirm_New_Password field blank
4. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on Current_Password, New_Password, and Confirm_New_Password fields indicating they are required; the form does not submit; credentials are not updated and the page does not display 'Password changed successfully.'

test_cases.test_case: New password violates strong-password policy
preconditions: User is on the Security Settings page and the Change Password form is visible
steps: 1. Enter <correct current password> in the Current_Password field
2. Enter <password that violates strong-password policy> in the New_Password field
3. Enter the same <password that violates strong-password policy> in the Confirm_New_Password field
4. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: New_Password field displays an inline validation error indicating it must comply with the strong-password policy; the form does not submit; credentials are not updated and the page does not display 'Password changed successfully.'

test_cases.test_case: Confirm_New_Password does not match New_Password
preconditions: User is on the Security Settings page and the Change Password form is visible
steps: 1. Enter <correct current password> in the Current_Password field
2. Enter <valid new password meeting policy> in the New_Password field
3. Enter <different value> in the Confirm_New_Password field
4. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Confirm_New_Password field displays an inline validation error indicating it must match New_Password; the form does not submit; credentials are not updated and the page does not display 'Password changed successfully.'

test_cases.test_case: Submit with incorrect Current_Password (precondition failure)
preconditions: User is on the Security Settings page and the Change Password form is visible
steps: 1. Enter <incorrect current password> in the Current_Password field
2. Enter <valid new password meeting policy> in the New_Password field
3. Enter the same <valid new password meeting policy> in the Confirm_New_Password field
4. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Current_Password field displays an inline error indicating the current password is incorrect; the form does not submit; credentials are not updated and the page does not display 'Password changed successfully.'

test_cases.test_case: Confirm password contains visually-identical but differently-normalized Unicode; match check
preconditions: User is logged in
User's Current_Password is known and is the correct current credential
steps: 1. Expand the Change_Password_Panel if it is collapsed
2. Enter <correct current password> into Current_Password
3. Enter <New password using composed Unicode characters> into New_Password
4. Enter <Visually identical string using decomposed Unicode characters> into Confirm_New_Password
5. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked; Confirm_New_Password field is highlighted and displays a field-level validation message indicating it does not match New_Password (per 'must match New_Password'); credentials are not updated

test_cases.test_case: Very long New_Password (200+ characters) against strong-password policy
preconditions: User is logged in
User's Current_Password is known and is the correct current credential
steps: 1. Expand the Change_Password_Panel if it is collapsed
2. Enter <correct current password> into Current_Password
3. Enter <very long string (200+ characters)> into New_Password
4. Enter the same <very long string (200+ characters)> into Confirm_New_Password
5. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked; New_Password field is highlighted and displays a field-level validation message indicating it violates the strong-password policy (per 'must comply with strong-password policy'); credentials are not updated

test_cases.test_case: Leading/trailing whitespace in New_Password and Confirm_New_Password
preconditions: User is logged in
User's Current_Password is known and is the correct current credential
steps: 1. Expand the Change_Password_Panel if it is collapsed
2. Enter <correct current password> into Current_Password
3. Enter <strong-password-with-leading-and-trailing-whitespace> into New_Password
4. Enter the identical value including leading/trailing whitespace into Confirm_New_Password
5. Click the Change Password button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; 'Password changed successfully.' is shown (succeeds). The system trims leading/trailing whitespace before applying the strong-password policy such that the trimmed value is accepted and credentials are updated

test_cases.test_case: Browser back then resubmit with old Current_Password after successful change
preconditions: User is logged in
User's Current_Password is known and is the correct current credential
steps: 1. Expand the Change_Password_Panel if it is collapsed
2. Enter <correct current password> into Current_Password
3. Enter <valid new password complying with policy> into New_Password
4. Enter the same <valid new password> into Confirm_New_Password
5. Click the Change Password button
6. Observe the 'Password changed successfully.' on screen
7. Press the browser Back button to return to the change password form
8. Without changing any fields (Current_Password still contains the old password), click the Change Password button again
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission is blocked; Current_Password field is highlighted and displays a verification error indicating the Current_Password is incorrect (per 'Current_Password must be correct'); no further credential change occurs

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-submit of Send Message does not create duplicate tickets
preconditions: User is logged in
Support Center page is open
Secure Message form is visible and Message_Body is filled
steps: 1. Click into the Message_Body field
2. Enter valid text into the Message_Body field
3. Click the Send Message button
4. Immediately click the Send Message button again before the first submission completes
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission attempt is blocked; UI shows a single 'Message sent successfully.' confirmation with one ticket ID and only one ticket is created (second attempt is blocked / error shown if duplicate prevented).
